title: Imitation Of Catullus. To Himself.
content:
В В В В В В В В В В В В Miser Catulle, desinas ineptire, etc.


В В В В Cease the sighing fool to play;
В В В В Cease to trifle life away;
В В В В Nor vainly think those joys thine own,
В В В В Which all, alas, have falsely flown.
В В В В What hours, Catullus, once were thine.
В В В В How fairly seemed thy day to shine,
В В В В When lightly thou didst fly to meet
В В В В The girl whose smile was then so sweet--
В В В В The girl thou lovedst with fonder pain
В В В В Than e'er thy heart can feel again.

В В В В В В В В Ye met--your souls seemed all in one,
В В В В Like tapers that commingling shone;
В В В В Thy heart was warm enough for both,
В В В В And hers, in truth, was nothing loath.

В В В В В В В В Such were the hours that once were thine;
В В В В But, ah! those hours no longer shine.
В В В В For now the nymph delights no more
В В В В In what she loved so much before;
В В В В And all Catullus now can do,
В В В В Is to be proud and frigid too;

В В В В Nor follow where the wanton flies,
В В В В Nor sue the bliss that she denies.
В В В В False maid! he bids farewell to thee,
В В В В To love, and all love's misery;
В В В В The heyday of his heart is o'er,
В В В В Nor will he court one favor more.

В В В В В В В В Fly, perjured girl!--but whither fly?
В В В В Who now will praise thy cheek and eye?
В В В В Who now will drink the syren tone,
В В В В Which tells him thou art all his own?
В В В В Oh, none:--and he who loved before
В В В В Can never, never love thee more.

В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *В В В В В В В В В В В В  *

В В В В В В В В В В В В "Neither do I condemn thee; go, and sin no more!"
В В В В В В В В В В В В --ST. JOHN, chap. viii.

В В В В Oh woman, if through sinful wile
В В В В В В В В Thy soul hath strayed from honor's track,
В В В В 'Tis mercy only can beguile,
В В В В В В В В By gentle ways, the wanderer back.

В В В В The stain that on thy virtue lies,
В В В В В В В В Washed by those tears, not long will stay;
В В В В As clouds that sully morning skies
В В В В В В В В May all be wept in showers away.

В В В В Go, go, be innocent,--and live;
В В В В В В В В The tongues of men may wound thee sore;
В В В В But Heaven in pity can forgive,
В В В В В В В В And bids thee "go, and sin no more!"
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Thomas Moore delivers a powerful performance in "Imitation Of Catullus. To Himself."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
